acceptance_criteria: No persisted artifact or served dashboard response contains raw API keys, common token patterns, private key blocks, configured secret values, raw command text, raw environment values, unsafe absolute paths, omission placeholders, raw artifact bodies, raw manifests, raw diff bodies, validation output payloads, planner handoff payloads, or attacker-controlled denied path payloads.
All docs, run artifact, manifest, log, planner, Codex event, diff, eval, validation, and dashboard-visible writes are redacted before persistence.
Traversal attempts using relative, absolute, encoded, or symlinked paths cannot read or write outside the configured workspace or allowed run artifact roots.
jj run accepts only plan files that resolve inside the configured workspace after documented path resolution, canonicalization, and symlink evaluation.

External, traversal, absolute-escape, encoded-escape, symlink-escape, unreadable, malformed, and secret-looking plan inputs are rejected before content read or artifact persistence with deterministic sanitized diagnostics.
In-workspace relative, process-cwd-resolved, and absolute in-workspace plan paths continue to work in dry-run and non-dry-run modes.
Plan input symlink swaps between validation and readback are rejected without reading, persisting, or rendering out-of-workspace content.
Codex event and summary artifacts are revalidated through the run artifact store before fallback creation, redaction, or readback, and symlink replacement cannot cause out-of-root reads or writes.
Low-information configured sensitive literals such as true, false, null, and none do not become global redaction values.
jj serve can display intended docs and run artifacts through guarded paths only, including dashboard, detail, history, comparison, and audit export routes.

Duplicate hostile query values for guarded run ID or path parameters on jj serve run detail, run progress, run status, run finish, doc, and artifact routes are denied deterministically without reflecting secret-looking or traversal payloads.
Command records include only sanitized argv/status/timing/result metadata and do not persist raw command text or raw environment dumps.
Persisted manifests and general JSON artifacts use safe path labels for workspace, run, plan, input, config, git, and repository paths and do not store raw workspace, plan, or external config paths.
Persisted manifest validation command entries do not contain raw command text.
Persisted git baseline artifacts are sanitized before write and do not leak raw workspace paths.

Manifest and dashboard security diagnostics expose only counts, booleans, root labels, relative-safe roots, sanitization status, dry-run parity status, sanitized failure categories, sanitized validation output redaction evidence, and sanitized git diff redaction evidence.
Denied path attempts are represented as sanitized categories and counts, not attacker-controlled path payloads.

Git diff artifacts persisted by jj contain only redacted content and no raw secrets, token-like values, private key blocks, raw command text, raw environment values, unsafe absolute paths, omission placeholders, raw manifests, raw artifact bodies, raw diff bodies, or attacker-controlled denied payloads.
Any git diff redaction evidence report or manifest fields contain only booleans, counts, sanitized categories, and safe relative artifact labels.
Validation stdout and stderr artifacts persisted by jj contain path-sanitized content using safe run and workspace labels and record redaction evidence without raw paths or sensitive payloads.
jj serve detail, history, compare, and audit export derive diff availability and redaction evidence through shared sanitized DTO/helper paths and never render raw diff bodies or unsafe paths.

Dry-run and non-dry-run produce equivalent git diff capture, redaction, diagnostics, manifest metadata, validation output sanitization, planner handoff metadata sanitization, safe path label persistence, and serve-visible behavior.
Dry-run and non-dry-run apply identical plan path validation, redaction, path-boundary, artifact persistence, manifest metadata, security diagnostic, and denial behavior.

Planner request, Codex fallback prompt, agent handoff JSON, planning context snapshot, task proposal reason, event, manifest, or log fields do not persist raw secrets, token-like values, private key blocks, raw command text, raw environment values, unsafe paths, omission placeholders, raw diff bodies, raw validation payloads, raw manifests, or attacker-controlled denied path payloads.
Planner and fallback handoff metadata contains only sanitized categories, counts, booleans, provider labels, mode labels, and safe relative artifact labels.

Prompt and context construction uses shared guarded DTO or sanitizer helpers at boundary points instead of ad hoc raw string assembly from manifests, command output, validation output, diffs, environment values, or denied path inputs.
jj serve dashboard links recent runs to guarded per-run detail pages.

A valid run detail page displays run status, timestamps, planner/provider metadata, dry-run mode, generated doc links, artifact availability, evaluation result, Codex result metadata, sanitized command metadata, disclosure-safe security diagnostics, sanitized git diff redaction evidence, and safe next-action hints when available.
Missing diagnostics render as unknown or unavailable states without errors or unsafe fallback fields.

Missing manifests, malformed manifests, missing artifacts, older manifests, denied inputs, and unavailable diagnostics render safe deterministic states without server errors or unsafe fallback fields.
jj serve dashboard links to a guarded run history page.
The guarded run history page lists discoverable runs newest first using sanitized manifest-derived fields only and links each row to the guarded run detail route.
Run history filters are allowlisted, deterministic, and do not reflect attacker-controlled invalid query values.
The run history page does not render raw secrets, raw commands, raw environment values, unsafe absolute paths, omission placeholders, raw diff bodies, or attacker-controlled denied path payloads.
Traversal, absolute, encoded, or symlinked path attempts cannot influence which run directories are listed or linked from run history.
Secret-looking or token-like run IDs are rejected for artifact store and jj serve run routes without reflecting rejected values.
jj serve exposes a guarded run comparison page for exactly two requested run IDs.
The run comparison page renders only sanitized manifest-derived fields and guarded links for both compared runs.

Invalid, missing, malformed, partial, older, identical, traversal, absolute, encoded, symlinked, secret-looking, or token-like run inputs render deterministic unavailable or denied states without server errors.

The run comparison page does not render raw secrets, token-like values, raw commands, raw environment values, unsafe absolute paths, omission placeholders, raw artifact contents, raw manifest contents, raw diff bodies, or attacker-controlled denied path payloads.
Run history or run detail pages provide safe compare entry points derived only from validated run IDs.
jj serve exposes a guarded sanitized JSON audit export endpoint for exactly one requested run ID.

Valid audit exports are derived only from sanitized run detail or dashboard DTO fields and include run status, timestamps, dry-run mode, planner/provider metadata, doc and artifact availability, evaluation state, Codex metadata, sanitized command metadata, security diagnostics, sanitized git diff redaction evidence, and safe next-action hints when available.
Run detail pages link to the audit export endpoint only for validated run IDs through guarded routes.

Missing, malformed, partial, older, traversal, absolute, encoded, symlinked, secret-looking, or token-like audit export inputs return deterministic unavailable or denied JSON without server errors or raw path/query reflection.

The audit export response contains no raw secrets, token-like values, raw command text, raw environment values, unsafe absolute paths, omission placeholders, raw artifact contents, raw manifests, raw diff bodies, or attacker-controlled denied path payloads.
Run detail, run history, run comparison, and audit export handlers obtain run metadata through one shared sanitized DTO construction path or clearly factored shared helpers.
Handlers do not read raw manifests, raw artifacts, raw commands, raw environment values, raw diffs, or filesystem paths directly after route input parsing except through guarded helper APIs.
Focused Go regression tests prove consistent sanitized fields and denial semantics across detail, history, compare, and audit export for valid and unsafe inputs.

Security behavior is covered by focused Go tests for redaction, path guards, artifact writing, manifest scrubbing, command metadata, dry-run and non-dry-run parity, diagnostics, serve access, run inspection access, Codex artifact symlink rejection, low-information configured literal filtering, secret-looking run ID denial, guarded plan input handling, sanitized validation output handling, git diff redaction evidence, and planner/Codex handoff sanitization.
Focused Go tests cover needs_work as a terminal web-run status to prevent validation completion races.

Auto or balanced mode resolves to bugfix only for explicit positive current evidence including validation failed, tests failed, failed_count greater than zero, failed or blocked runnable task, provider failure, panic, fatal error, or regression.

Auto or balanced mode does not resolve to bugfix for healthy or negated evidence including no blocker, not blocked, no validation failed, validation passed, failed_count: 0, status passed, tests pass, no regressions found, all tasks done, or no runnable tasks.
Concrete feature, security, hardening, quality, and docs selections are overridden to bugfix only when the same positive critical blocker evidence exists.

Resolution reasons, events, manifests, and planning context remain sanitized and do not include raw commands, raw environment values, secrets, token-like values, unsafe paths, omission placeholders, or raw attacker-controlled payloads.
Existing security, hardening, quality, docs, and feature classification behavior remains covered and unchanged when no real blocker exists.
Focused Go tests cover positive blocker evidence and negated or healthy evidence side by side.

A fixture-driven Go security gate covers persisted run artifacts, manifest scrubbing, command metadata, Codex artifacts, shared run DTOs, jj serve detail/history/compare/audit surfaces, guarded plan input handling, accepted plan input parity, denied plan input non-persistence, unsafe inputs, sanitized validation failure artifacts and metadata, git diff redaction evidence, planner/Codex handoff boundaries, duplicate hostile serve query values, and dry-run/non-dry-run parity.

Traversal, absolute, encoded, symlinked, malformed, missing, partial, older, identical, secret-looking, and token-like inputs produce deterministic denied or unavailable states without raw value reflection.

Rendered HTML, audit JSON, manifests, checked artifacts, validation artifacts, Codex events, Codex summaries, planner/fallback metadata, and git diff artifacts contain no raw secrets, token-like values, private key blocks, raw command text, raw environment values, unsafe absolute paths, omission placeholders, raw artifact bodies, raw manifests, raw diff bodies, or attacker-controlled denied path payloads.
Tests prove route handlers use shared guarded DTO/helper paths rather than handler-local raw manifest, artifact, command, environment, diff, or path reads after route input parsing.
Dry-run and non-dry-run fixtures produce equivalent redaction, boundary, diagnostic, validation output, planner handoff metadata, git diff redaction behavior, and safe path label persistence.
Failure output exposes only sanitized categories, counts, booleans, and safe relative labels.
Untrusted or partial manifests do not render free-form validation reason or summary text on served run inspection surfaces.

Fixtures create valid runs, then mutate manifest fields, artifact references, validation summaries, command metadata, diff metadata, provider fields, timestamps, and next-action hints with secret-looking values, unsafe paths, traversal strings, encoded escapes, raw command text, raw environment-looking values, private key blocks, omission placeholders, and attacker-controlled denial payloads.

jj serve detail, history, compare, and audit export routes render deterministic denied, unknown, unavailable, or sanitized states for stale, partial, malformed, and internally inconsistent run metadata without reflecting mutated payloads.
Valid untampered runs continue to render expected sanitized fields and guarded links.
Hardened run inspection DTOs sanitize unsafe category text, guarded artifact labels, validation counts, and command argv display values before detail, history, compare, or audit export rendering.
scripts/validate.sh is documented as the single release validation entry point.
The release validation entry point runs the focused internal/serve and internal/run security boundary suites, full tests, vet, CLI build, and diff-check.

The release validation entry point exits non-zero on any security boundary, redaction, path guard, artifact, planner handoff, diff, validation output, dashboard, test, vet, build, or diff-check regression.
The release validation entry point suppresses child command output and prints only deterministic release-gate metadata with fixed labels, categories, booleans, counts, and exit codes.
A Go regression test using fake child tools proves hostile child output is not reflected while required release validation steps are invoked.

No raw secrets, token-like values, private key blocks, raw command text, raw environment values, unsafe absolute paths, raw manifests, raw artifact bodies, raw diff bodies, validation payloads, planner handoff payloads, or attacker-controlled denied payloads are printed or persisted by the validation entry point.
No completed task is reopened unless the validation entry point finds a concrete failing disclosure or boundary case.

No new dashboard pages, CLI flags, raw export endpoints, broad file import behavior, scanners, redaction algorithm expansion, or unrelated user-facing behavior are added by the security validation follow-up.
go test ./internal/serve, go test ./internal/run, go test ./..., go vet ./..., go build -o jj ./cmd/jj, ./scripts/validate.sh, and git diff --check pass.
A CI job runs ./scripts/validate.sh for security-relevant pull request or push changes.
The CI job fails when ./scripts/validate.sh exits non-zero.

The CI configuration does not enable verbose shell tracing, child output passthrough, raw artifact upload, raw manifest upload, raw diff upload, secret/environment echoing, or continue-on-error for the release gate.
CI output remains limited to the sanitized release-gate metadata emitted by scripts/validate.sh plus standard CI step status.
Existing release validation documentation states that scripts/validate.sh is both the local and CI release gate.
No completed task is reopened and no unrelated user-facing feature is added by CI release gate enforcement.

No raw secrets, token-like values, private key blocks, raw command text, raw environment values, unsafe absolute paths, raw manifests, raw artifact bodies, raw diff bodies, validation payloads, planner handoff payloads, or attacker-controlled denied payloads are printed or persisted by the validation entry point or CI wrapper.
goals: Generate concrete specs and tasks from a single plan file while preserving an auditable run history.

Keep all generated artifacts, served files, plan inputs, workspace mutations, validation outputs, git diff artifacts, planner and Codex handoff metadata, and run inspection surfaces inside the resolved target workspace and guarded run artifact roots.

Prevent secrets, credentials, private key material, omission placeholders, raw commands, raw environment values, unsafe paths, raw manifests, raw artifact bodies, raw diff bodies, validation output payloads, planner handoff payloads, and attacker-controlled denied payloads from being persisted or rendered.
Ensure jj serve provides a dashboard-first local experience with guarded run detail, history, comparison, and sanitized audit export views.
Preserve dry-run and non-dry-run workflows while applying identical redaction, path-boundary, diagnostics, artifact persistence, diff redaction, validation, and planner handoff metadata behavior.
Ensure auto and balanced task proposal resolution selects bugfix only from positive current blocker evidence and does not loop on healthy, negated, or completed-task context.

Enforce plan input path handling so explicitly provided plans cannot escape the configured workspace or leak external sensitive content through artifacts, manifests, logs, events, planner context, eval output, Codex summaries, validation output, git diff artifacts, or dashboard surfaces.
Persist git diff artifacts only after redaction and expose any diff redaction evidence through sanitized metadata only.
Lock completed security guardrails with deterministic release validation that patches only confirmed disclosure or boundary regressions.
Treat stale, partial, malformed, manually edited, or internally inconsistent run metadata as untrusted on served run inspection surfaces unless fields are sanitized through guarded DTO helpers.
Persist manifest and general JSON metadata using safe workspace, run, or path labels so raw workspace, plan, and external config paths are not stored.
Treat needs_work web-run status as terminal so validation completion does not race against further status polling.
Use scripts/validate.sh as the single documented release validation entry point for completed security guardrails and standard release checks.
Ensure the existing sanitized release validation entry point is enforced automatically by repository CI for security-relevant changes.
non_goals: Provide a multi-user dashboard or authentication system.
Replace git review or guarantee AI output correctness.
Add unrelated user-facing features while unresolved security risks remain.

Expose raw commands, environment dumps, secret values, sensitive absolute paths, private key material, token-like strings, omission placeholders, raw artifact bodies, raw manifests, raw diff bodies, validation output payloads, planner handoff payloads, raw prompt exports, or attacker-controlled denied path payloads.

Add new security scanners or expand redaction algorithms as part of run history, run detail, run comparison, run audit export, guarded plan input handling, validation output handling, git diff evidence handling, planner handoff hardening, tampered run metadata hardening, or security validation follow-up work.
Add client-side full-text search over raw artifacts or manifests.
Add raw artifact diffing or full-text document comparison as part of the guarded run comparison page.
Add raw manifest export, raw artifact export, raw diff export, raw prompt export, or artifact content bundling as part of the guarded run audit JSON export.

Add new dashboard pages or CLI flags as part of guarded plan input hardening, validation output handling, git diff evidence policy, planner handoff hardening, tampered run metadata hardening, or security validation follow-up work.
Add new product features while fixing automatic task proposal blocker classification.
Allow explicitly provided plan files outside the workspace by default.
Reopen completed tasks without fresh failing validation, tests, or confirmed regression evidence.
Expose child command stdout or stderr from the release validation entry point; release validation reports sanitized release-gate metadata only.
Add new security scanners or expand redaction algorithms as part of release gate automation.
Add new dashboard pages, CLI flags, raw export endpoints, raw artifact uploads, raw manifest uploads, raw diff uploads, or unrelated user-facing behavior as part of CI release gate enforcement.
requirements: jj run must read a non-empty Markdown plan from the explicitly provided path using documented path resolution behavior, then require the resolved plan file to remain inside the configured workspace boundary.
The resolved cwd value, or process cwd when cwd is absent, is the workspace boundary for generated docs, run artifacts, workspace mutations, and accepted plan input files.

Plan path validation must canonicalize requested paths, reject unsupported encoded traversal and escape forms, evaluate symlinks where applicable, reject traversal attempts, and reject symlink or absolute-path escapes outside the workspace before reading content.
Plan input files must be revalidated through the same guarded path result immediately before readback so symlink replacement cannot cause out-of-workspace reads or persistence.

Rejected external, traversal, absolute-escape, encoded-escape, symlink-escape, unreadable, malformed, or secret-looking plan inputs must produce deterministic sanitized errors without reflecting raw paths, raw OS errors, secrets, token-like values, omission placeholders, or attacker-controlled payloads.
In-workspace relative plans, process-cwd-resolved plans, and absolute in-workspace plans must continue to work in dry-run and non-dry-run modes after canonicalization and symlink evaluation.

Accepted plan content, planner output, Codex output, Codex summaries, command summaries, evaluation output, validation output, docs writes, manifest serialization, event writes, run artifact writes, git diff artifacts, and dashboard render data must pass through shared redaction before persistence or display.
Manifest and general JSON persistence must sanitize display strings with run and workspace roots, preserving only safe workspace, run, relative, or generic path labels for stored paths.

Persisted manifests must label cwd as the workspace, sanitize plan path, input path, config file, Codex binary, git root, repository directory, and git baseline path fields, and strip raw validation command text.
Persisted git baseline text must be sanitized before artifact writes so raw workspace paths are not stored.

Planner request payloads, Codex fallback prompts, agent handoff JSON, planning context snapshots, task proposal reasons, and planner/fallback metadata must use shared sanitization helpers before being persisted, logged, rendered, or represented in manifests or events.

Prompt and context builders must not directly concatenate raw manifests, command output, validation payloads, diff bodies, environment values, denied path inputs, or unsafe artifact bodies into persisted handoff metadata.
Planner and fallback diagnostics may expose only booleans, counts, sanitized categories, provider labels, mode labels, and safe relative artifact labels.

Validation stdout and stderr artifacts must be path-sanitized before persistence using safe run and workspace labels, and validation manifests or metadata must record only sanitized redaction evidence.

Git diff artifacts must be redacted in place before persistence; optional redaction reports or manifest fields may include only booleans, counts, sanitized category labels, and safe relative artifact labels.

Dry-run and non-dry-run modes must apply identical plan path validation, redaction, path-boundary, artifact persistence, git diff capture, diff redaction, manifest metadata, security diagnostics, validation output sanitization, planner handoff metadata sanitization, and denial behavior.
Path guards must canonicalize requested paths, evaluate symlinks where applicable, and reject traversal, absolute-path escape, encoded escape, and symlink escape outside allowed roots.

jj serve must only serve files from guarded workspace and .jj/runs roots and must reject traversal, absolute, encoded, secret-looking, token-like, or symlink escape inputs with deterministic denied or unavailable states.

Guarded jj serve handlers that accept run IDs or paths, including run detail, run progress, run status, run finish, doc, and artifact handlers, must parse those request parameters through shared single-value helpers and deny duplicate hostile query values deterministically without reflecting rejected values.
Web run completion handling must treat needs_work as a terminal status.
Command execution records must persist sanitized argv, cwd labels, status, duration, and result metadata only; raw command text and raw environment values must not be stored or rendered.

Manifest and dashboard security diagnostics may expose only counts, booleans, root labels, relative-safe roots, sanitization status, dry-run parity status, sanitized failure categories, validation output redaction evidence, and sanitized git diff redaction evidence.
Denied path attempts must be represented as sanitized categories and counts, not attacker-controlled path payloads.
Security failures must be actionable without exposing sensitive payloads.
OpenAI API planning, Codex CLI fallback planning, dry-run, non-dry-run, and dashboard flows must continue to work after redaction and boundary enforcement.

Codex event and summary outputs must resolve under the guarded run artifact root before launch and must be revalidated through the artifact store before fallback creation, redaction, or readback; symlink replacement of those outputs must be rejected without reading outside content.
Configured sensitive literals used for redaction must ignore low-information values such as true, false, null, and none so they do not become global redaction traps.
jj serve dashboard recent runs must link to guarded per-run detail pages.
jj serve run detail pages must resolve run IDs only under the guarded .jj/runs root, render sanitized manifest-derived metadata, and link to intended docs and artifacts only through guarded routes.

Run detail pages must display safe deterministic states for missing manifests, malformed manifests, missing artifacts, and older manifests without diagnostics, without raw path, command, env, secret, or payload disclosure.
jj serve must expose a guarded run history page that lists discoverable runs under the guarded .jj/runs root sorted newest first.
The guarded run history page must support allowlisted filters for run status, dry-run mode, planner provider, evaluation state, and run-id substring query without reflecting invalid raw query values.
Run history rows must be derived from sanitized manifest or dashboard DTO fields and link only to guarded run detail routes.
Missing, malformed, partial, and older manifests in run history must render as unknown or unavailable without server errors.
Traversal, absolute, encoded, or symlinked path attempts must not influence which run directories are listed or linked from run history.
Run IDs may contain only letters, numbers, dots, underscores, and dashes, must not use reserved traversal-like forms, and must be rejected when they match configured secrets or common token patterns.
Run ID validation failures must use generic deterministic errors that do not echo rejected run ID values.
jj serve must expose a guarded run comparison page for exactly two requested run IDs.
The run comparison page must resolve both run IDs only beneath the guarded .jj/runs root using existing path guards and sanitized run DTO construction.

The run comparison page must render side-by-side sanitized status, timestamps, dry-run mode, planner/provider metadata, generated doc availability, artifact availability, evaluation state, Codex result metadata, command metadata, and disclosure-safe security diagnostic summaries.

Invalid, missing, identical, malformed, partial, older, traversal, absolute, encoded, or symlinked run inputs must render deterministic unavailable or denied states without server errors or raw query reflection.
Run history or run detail pages must provide safe compare entry points derived only from validated run IDs and guarded routes.
jj serve must expose a guarded sanitized JSON audit export endpoint for exactly one requested run ID.
The run audit export must resolve the requested run ID only beneath the guarded .jj/runs root using existing path guards and sanitized run DTO construction.

Valid run audit exports must be derived only from sanitized run detail or dashboard DTO fields and include run status, timestamps, dry-run mode, planner/provider metadata, generated doc and artifact availability, evaluation state, Codex metadata, sanitized command metadata, disclosure-safe security diagnostics, sanitized git diff redaction evidence, and safe next-action hints when available.
Run detail pages must link to the run audit export endpoint only for validated run IDs through guarded routes.

Missing, malformed, partial, older, traversal, absolute, encoded, or symlinked run inputs for audit export must return deterministic unavailable or denied JSON without server errors or raw path/query reflection.

The run audit export must not include raw artifact contents, raw manifest contents, raw diff bodies, raw secrets, token-like values, raw command text, raw environment values, unsafe absolute paths, omission placeholders, or attacker-controlled denied path payloads.
Run detail, run history, run comparison, and run audit export handlers must obtain run metadata through one shared sanitized DTO construction path or clearly factored shared helpers.

Route handlers must not perform handler-local raw manifest parsing, raw artifact reads, raw command rendering, raw environment rendering, raw diff rendering, or ad hoc denied-state mapping after route input parsing except through guarded helper APIs.

Shared run metadata construction must consistently populate status, timestamps, dry-run mode, planner/provider metadata, generated doc availability, artifact availability, evaluation state, Codex metadata, sanitized command metadata, disclosure-safe security diagnostics, sanitized git diff redaction evidence, and safe next-action hints where available.
Safe detail, history, compare, and audit links must be generated only from validated run IDs and guarded route helpers.
Existing public routes and JSON response shapes should remain compatible unless a field is currently unsafe or inconsistent.

Untrusted, stale, partial, malformed, manually edited, or internally inconsistent manifest fields, artifact references, validation summaries, command metadata, diff metadata, provider fields, timestamps, and next-action hints must render only deterministic denied, unknown, unavailable, or sanitized states on detail, history, compare, and audit export surfaces.

Unsafe manifest-derived category text, artifact labels, validation counts, command argv display values, provider metadata, timestamps, diff metadata, and next-action hints must be sanitized or normalized before display or JSON export.

Auto or balanced task proposal resolution must choose bugfix only from positive current evidence of failed validation, failed tests, failed or blocked runnable tasks, provider failure, panic, fatal error, or regression.

Healthy or negated evidence such as no blocker, not blocked, validation passed, tests pass, failed_count: 0, status passed, no regressions found, all tasks done, or no runnable tasks must not trigger bugfix resolution.
Concrete feature, security, hardening, quality, or docs selections must be overridden to bugfix only when the same positive critical blocker evidence exists.

Task proposal resolution reasons, events, manifests, and planning context must remain sanitized and must not echo raw logs, paths, commands, environment values, secrets, token-like values, omission placeholders, or attacker-controlled payloads.
Untrusted or partial manifest validation reason and summary text must not render on served run inspection surfaces unless the manifest is trusted.

A consolidated fixture-driven security release-gate validation path must exercise hostile fixtures across persisted run artifacts, manifest scrubbing, command metadata, Codex artifacts, shared run DTOs, run detail, history, compare, audit export, unsafe run IDs, path denial, accepted and denied guarded plan input handling, sanitized validation failure artifacts and metadata, git diff redaction evidence, planner and Codex handoff payloads, tampered run metadata, duplicate hostile serve query values, and dry-run/non-dry-run parity.
scripts/validate.sh must remain the single documented release validation entry point for release-blocking security validation.
The release validation entry point must run the existing focused serve and run security boundary suites, the full Go test suite, vet, CLI build, and diff-check.

The release validation entry point must suppress child command stdout and stderr, emit only deterministic sanitized metadata with fixed step labels, categories, booleans, counts, and exit codes, and exit non-zero when any step fails.
Release validation regression coverage must prove hostile child output is not reflected while the required validation steps are invoked.

Release-gate assertions must check rendered HTML, audit JSON, manifests, persisted checked artifacts, validation artifacts, events, summaries, planner/fallback metadata, and git diff artifacts for absence of raw secrets, token-like values, private key blocks, raw command text, raw environment values, unsafe absolute paths, omission placeholders, raw artifact bodies, raw manifests, raw diff bodies, and attacker-controlled denied path payloads.
Release-gate failure output must expose only sanitized categories, counts, booleans, and safe relative labels.
CI configuration must invoke scripts/validate.sh for pull requests or pushes that affect Go code, scripts, docs/spec/task metadata, security fixtures, or release validation behavior.
CI must fail when scripts/validate.sh exits non-zero and must not mask the gate with continue-on-error behavior.
CI wrappers must not enable verbose shell tracing, child output passthrough, raw artifact upload, raw manifest upload, raw diff upload, secret echoing, or environment dumps.
CI output must remain limited to sanitized scripts/validate.sh release-gate metadata plus standard CI step status.
Existing release validation documentation must state that scripts/validate.sh is both the local and CI release gate.

Completed security guardrails must remain closed unless scripts/validate.sh, focused Go security suites, full tests, vet, build, diff-check, or the CI release gate produce a concrete disclosure, path-boundary, artifact, dashboard, planner handoff, validation output, git diff, command metadata, or release-gate output regression.

Task proposal reasons, events, manifests, planning context, and CI diagnostics must remain sanitized and must not echo raw logs, paths, commands, environment values, secrets, token-like values, omission placeholders, manifests, artifacts, diffs, validation payloads, or attacker-controlled payloads.
summary: jj is a CLI that turns a planning file into implementation-ready specs, tasks, Codex runs, validation artifacts, and a local dashboard. Security hardening remains the core constraint: generated artifacts, served run-inspection surfaces, explicit plan inputs, command metadata, manifests, validation outputs, git diff artifacts, planner and Codex handoff metadata, and run metadata are guarded, redacted, and kept inside the configured workspace or allowed run artifact roots. jj serve includes guarded dashboard, run detail, run history, run comparison, and sanitized audit export flows, while automatic task proposal resolution selects bugfix only from positive current blocker evidence. TASK-0035 added validated persistence hardening so manifest and general JSON writes use safe path labels, validation command text is stripped from persisted manifests, git baseline text is sanitized before artifact writes, and dry-run and non-dry-run fixtures prove raw workspace, plan, and external config paths are not persisted. Git diff evidence policy is resolved as redacted-in-place persisted diff artifacts plus optional sanitized metadata containing only booleans, counts, categories, and safe relative labels. Release-gate regression coverage locks accepted and denied guarded plan input behavior across dry-run and full-run flows, sanitized validation failure artifacts, disclosure-safe validation metadata, stale or tampered run metadata, planner/Codex handoff payload boundaries, guarded duplicate-query serve route parsing for run and path inputs, and TASK-0036 release validation. scripts/validate.sh is the documented single local and CI release validation gate: it runs the focused serve and run security boundary suites, the full Go test suite, vet, CLI build, and diff-check; suppresses child output; emits only fixed sanitized release-gate metadata; and exits non-zero when any step fails. TASK-0037 ran the release validation entry point successfully with no confirmed disclosure or boundary regression. TASK-0038 configured repository CI to invoke the sanitized release gate for security-relevant pull request and push changes, without continue-on-error, verbose shell tracing, child output passthrough, raw artifact, manifest, or diff uploads, secret echoing, or environment dumps, and updated release validation documentation to identify scripts/validate.sh as both the local and CI gate.
title: jj Security Boundary and Dashboard Run Inspection
